name: visus-mcp
description:
Visus is a security-focused pre-filter that sanitizes web content before it enters Claude's context window. It reduces token usage by up to 70% on content-heavy pages, detects and neutralizes 7 categories of Indirect Prompt Injection attacks, redacts PII, and generates cryptographic proof that sanitization ran — before a single character reaches your conversation.

Built on the principle that model safety ≠ pipeline safety.

No API key required. Sanitization runs locally — web content never reaches external infrastructure.
long_description:
## Security-First Web Access for Claude

Visus is a pre-filter MCP server that applies defense-in-depth to web content **before** it reaches your conversation. Unlike other MCP web tools that pass raw content directly to the LLM, Visus runs every byte through a multi-stage security pipeline.

### What It Does

- **Threat Detection**: Scans for 7 categories of Indirect Prompt Injection (IPI) attacks mapped to OWASP LLM Top 10, NIST AI RMF, MITRE ATLAS, and ISO/IEC 42001:2023
- **PII Redaction**: Automatically removes emails, phone numbers, SSNs, credit cards, and IP addresses
- **Token Efficiency**: Reader mode strips nav bars, ads, and boilerplate — reducing token consumption by up to 70% on content-heavy pages
- **Cryptographic Proof**: Every response includes a SHA-256 + HMAC proof that sanitization ran, with framework mappings for compliance audits

### The Security Pipeline

1. **Fetch**: Playwright renders the page with full JavaScript execution
2. **IPI Detection**: 7 specialized detectors scan for instruction override, role hijacking, data exfiltration, tool abuse, context poisoning, encoded payloads, and steganographic attacks
3. **Pattern Neutralization**: 43 injection patterns are stripped or replaced with `[REDACTED:pattern_name]`
4. **PII Redaction**: Sensitive data replaced with `[REDACTED:PII_TYPE]`
5. **Proof Generation**: SHA-256 content hash + HMAC signature with framework compliance metadata
6. **Token Ceiling**: Content truncated at 24k tokens to prevent context exhaustion

### Trust Model

- Runs locally — no external API calls
- Open source (MIT License) — audit the code yourself
- No authentication required for open-source tier
- Deterministic output — same input always produces same sanitized result

### Known Limitations

- Semantic injection (benign-looking business language) not detected by pattern matching
- Multi-step cross-call chaining attacks not currently detected
- Adversarially crafted LLM-generated injection can bypass regex patterns
- Non-English injection partially covered

See [SECURITY.md](https://github.com/visus-mcp/visus-mcp/blob/main/SECURITY.md) for full threat model.

tools.name: visus_fetch
tools.description: Fetches a URL through a security pipeline: strips nav/ads/scripts, runs IPI threat detection (7 attack categories), sanitizes 43 injection patterns, redacts PII, applies 24k token ceiling. Returns clean content with cryptographic proof and threat_summary.

tools.name: visus_fetch_structured
tools.description: Fetches a URL and extracts structured data matching a user-defined schema, with full security pipeline applied before extraction.

tools.name: visus_read
tools.description: Reader mode — extracts article content only via Mozilla Readability, reducing tokens by ~70% on content-heavy pages. Full security pipeline applies.

tools.name: visus_search
tools.description: Sanitized DuckDuckGo web search. SEO spam and injection patterns are removed before results enter context. No API key required.

tools.name: visus_report
tools.description: Generates a structured compliance audit report from visus-mcp sanitization logs, including detected threat classifications and framework mappings (NIST AI RMF, OWASP LLM Top 10, ISO/IEC 42001).

tools.name: visus_verify
tools.description: Verify a sanitization proof record. Confirms that a request was processed by the Visus pipeline before content reached the LLM. Produces a compliance statement suitable for EU AI Act Art. 9/13 documentation and GDPR Art. 32 security evidence.
